steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage criticizes the energy ladder model as "reductive." It concedes that income "of course constrained fuel choice" but adds that several other factors also influenced decisions. The question asks what the author would most likely agree with about household income.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'can explain some but not all of the differences in fuel choice': matches perfectly — income mattered ("of course constrained") but other factors also played a role, so income is partial, not complete. ✓
  • Choice B — 'often said to influence fuel choice but actually does not': too strong — the author explicitly says income "of course constrained fuel choice," acknowledging a real effect. ✗
  • Choice C — 'affects fuel choice but not for the reasons assumed by the energy ladder model': the passage doesn't dispute the mechanism by which income affects choice, only that income alone is insufficient. ✗
  • Choice D — 'constrains the amount of fuel but not the type': the energy ladder model is specifically about fuel type (solid vs. high-technology), and the passage never introduces an amount vs. type distinction. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
